Manually break the JSON body before sending. Expect 400 with a generic parse-error message, no raw traceback.

DEMO NOTE: A real unhandled 500 was reproduced during development by temporarily adding `raise Exception('rollback test')` inside CompanyListCreateView.post(). Response was a generic 500 INTERNAL_ERROR with no internal detail leaked; full traceback was captured server-side in logs/app.log and the observability.RequestLog table. That line has since been removed. This request alone returns a normal 201 unless the exception is re-added temporarily.
Expect 201. Company + CompanyMember created together inside one @transaction.atomic block.

MANUAL DEMO: Temporarily add `raise Exception('rollback test')` right after `CompanyMember.objects.create(...)` in CompanyListCreateView.post(), restart the server, then send this request. Expect 500. Confirm via Django shell that Company.objects.filter(name='RollbackTestCo123').exists() is False, and no matching CompanyMember exists either. Remove the temporary line afterwards. Already verified once during development.

item.name: Check Correlation ID Header - Any Request

request.description: Expect 200. Check Response Headers for 'X-Correlation-ID' (a UUID), unique per request.

item.name: Trigger Error To Check Structured Log

request.description: Expect 404. Then check logs/app.log or observability.RequestLog in logs_db for a JSON entry with this request's correlation_id, level, path, status_code.
Expect 201. Then in Django shell: RequestLog.objects.using('logs_db').filter(path__icontains='register').last().request_body -- 'password' must show as '***REDACTED***'.
Expect 200. MANUAL DB CHECK: query information_schema.tables on logs_db connection; only 'request_logs' (and django_migrations) should exist there.
